sections.heading: Follow-Up
sections.content: After completion of primary treatment, patients enter a surveillance phase with regular clinical visits, imaging, and laboratory monitoring. The schedule is more intensive initially and decreases over time [OV-6]. Surveillance aims to detect recurrence early when it may be amenable to secondary cytoreduction or systemic therapy. Rising CA-125 or symptoms should prompt investigation [OV-6]. Recurrence can be local (pelvic), peritoneal, or distant. Platinum-sensitive recurrence occurs ≥6 months after prior chemotherapy, while platinum-resistant recurrence occurs <6 months or during therapy [OV-7].

timeline.title: Post-treatment Surveillance
timeline.body: Regular clinical visits, imaging, and laboratory monitoring. The schedule is more intensive initially and decreases over time [OV-6].

late_effects.name: Cardiovascular toxicity
late_effects.detail: Especially with anthracyclines or bevacizumab; monitor blood pressure, echocardiography as indicated [OV-D].

late_effects.name: Secondary malignancies
late_effects.detail: Long-term risk from platinum or radiation therapy; regular wellness care [OV-6].

late_effects.name: Fertility and sexual health
late_effects.detail: Address in survivorship; refer to REI for fertility preservation options [OV-1].

late_effects.name: Psychosocial distress
late_effects.detail: Screen and manage with supportive care [NCCN Guidelines for Distress Management].
